description:
Goal: close the arc cleanly and hand back a smaller migration problem. Signal: docs, telemetry guidance, and ticket text all describe the post-fork ownership cut precisely; `fz-rh2.9` is either newly tractable or obviously obsolete with any true remainder filed as its own ticket.

Change:
- update northstar, agent docs, and telemetry guidance to match the in-house backend boundary after the compiler2-only cleanup
- remove temporary scaffolding/fixtures introduced during the fork and simplification
- rewrite or retire stale migration tickets so the queue points at the real remaining work, if any

Why:
- this arc only pays off if it leaves the queue cleaner than it found it and deletes the temporary baggage it used to get there

Checks:
- no temporary fixtures remain without a removal ticket
- returning to `fz-rh2.9` is clearly the next move, or a smaller replacement ticket exists and explains why
title: Compiler2 native backends: close the arc and restate the migration problem
